meta.title: Shimukappu Central Ski Area — LP Mock (Zero-Revenue Ecosystem · Patronage Strategy)
meta.description: No chairlifts, no crowds, pure powder. Two rope tows only, free for villagers, 100% advanced skiers, 34-minute average stay. Contrast with nearby Tomamu mega-resort. Powder Patronage donations, Secret Powder Experience ¥30,000–50,000, Rope Tow Master certification, mobile fat-ski rental—LP mock based on the Shimukappu zero-revenue ecosystem strategy report.

banner.text: Rope tows only · patronage · Tomamu contrast · village ecosystem | AI-generated mock images

hero.title:
Shimukappu Central
Ski Area
hero.tagline: No chairlifts, no crowds, pure powder—two rope tows, 100% advanced skiers, a hidden gem beside Tomamu.

transit.title:
Next to a mega-resort,
rope-tow pure powder.
transit.lead: One step off the bustle of adjacent Tomamu Resort—about 3 km along a narrow levee road from National Route 237, the same distance from Shimukappu Station. A national ski hill with no lifts and no crowds: 100% advanced skiers and a 34-minute average stay—compact, intense Japow. Reachable by rental car or taxi, and the launch point for Secret Powder Experience tours from Tomamu guests.
transit.imgAlt: Beyond Tomamu mega-resort—a rope-tow-only pure powder hill at the end of a narrow levee road

primary.title: No chairlifts · no crowds · pure powder
primary.lead: Two rope tows only—no chairlifts, no lift lines, 100% advanced skiers on untracked burn. GS 620 m and SL 420 m courses, max 1,000 m length, 25° pitch. A 34-minute average stay means ski short, ski deep—the polar opposite of Tomamu's lift queues at Shimukappu Central National Ski Area.
primary.imgAlt: Uncrowded rope-tow powder burn—100% advanced skiers on pure Japow terrain

secondary.title: Free for villagers, patronage for visitors
secondary.lead: Villagers ski free—visitors support the hill through digital donation QR for Powder Patronage (¥1,000/day or ¥10,000/season). A zero-lift-revenue model that protects local use while making respect for pure powder visible—a new pricing philosophy (LP concept).

patronage.title:
Villagers ski free,
visitors give back.
patronage.lead: Shimukappu Powder Patronage—digital donation QR at ¥1,000/day or ¥10,000/season. Villagers remain free. Sustain a zero-lift-revenue national ski hill through a "powder culture patronage" model—the core of the zero-revenue ecosystem strategy (LP concept).
patronage.noticeBody: 34-minute average stay, 100% advanced skiers—protect uncrowded pure powder as everyday village use while extending visitor ARPU to ¥6,700–19,000 through patronage, secret tours, and the village ecosystem.
patronage.imgAlt: Digital donation QR—a patronage model that keeps villagers skiing free

tour.title:
Secret Powder Experience,
¥30,000–50,000.
tour.lead: A premium tour for Tomamu guests—taxi transfer, rope-tow coaching, and pure powder laps packaged as one experience. A high-value line that safely guides mega-resort visitors to the adjacent national hidden gem (LP strategy).
tour.whisper: A flagship built on geographic proximity to Tomamu—beyond two rope tows, the core of differentiation and guest spend.
tour.imgAlt: Taxi from Tomamu to Secret Powder Experience—rope-tow coaching on pure powder

rope.title:
Rope Tow Master,
wooden tag ¥1,500–2,000.
rope.lead: Because this hill runs on rope tows alone—certify proper loading, timing, and advanced efficiency with the Rope Tow Master program. A wooden tag credential that brands Shimukappu Central's rope-tow culture (LP concept).
rope.imgAlt: Wooden-tag Rope Tow Master certification—the skill culture of a rope-tow-only hill

rental.title:
Mobile rental,
fat skis ¥6,000–8,000/day.
rental.lead: No fixed rental shop at this national hill—a mobile rental van delivers fat skis to the parking area along the levee road. Gear optimized for deep, untracked snow. A revenue complement for Powder Patronage visitors and secret-tour guests (LP concept).
rental.noticeBody: Capture equipment spend even on a 34-minute average stay—mobile infrastructure delivers a no-gear-carry advanced experience on a rope-tow-only hill.
rental.imgAlt: Mobile rental van—fat skis ready for deep powder terrain

ecosystem.title:
Road station, onsen,
Topeni Wakka distillery.
ecosystem.lead: Revenue the hill alone cannot generate—Shimukappu Village's food, onsen, and distillery ecosystem shapes an 11:00–17:30 journey and visitor ARPU of ¥6,700–19,000. Design stay reasons across the whole village for patronage visitors (LP strategy).
ecosystem.michiLead: Morning and lunch starting point—local-ingredient breakfast and midday meals to fuel up before the levee-road approach. The gateway to an 11:00 journey start.
ecosystem.onsenLead: Bathing ¥700—warm-up and recovery after runs. Melt the cold of pure powder in a forest onsen, a standard post-ski phase.
ecosystem.ginLead: Topeni Wakka maple syrup and Rupu no Mori gin—craft experiences distilled from Shimukappu's forest bounty. An evening village-brand and souvenir line.
ecosystem.imgAlt: Roadside Station Shizen Taikan Shimukappu—breakfast and lunch start the village ecosystem

journey.title:
Half a day,
¥6,700–19,000 across the village.
journey.lead: From road-station lunch through rope-tow powder, onsen, and distillery—bundle Shimukappu Village's ecosystem around a 34-minute-average hill into an 11:00–17:30 visitor revenue model (LP concept). Ideal as a half-day escape for Tomamu guests.

01.title: 11:00 Shizen Taikan Shimukappu
01.body: Breakfast and lunch. Local ingredients for fuel

02.title: 12:30 Shimukappu Stn. → hill
02.body: ~3 km levee road · narrow route from Route 237

03.title: 14:00 Rope-tow powder
03.body: GS 620 m + SL 420 m · 100% advanced · villagers free

06.title: 16:30 Topeni Wakka distillery
06.body: Maple syrup and Rupu no Mori gin experience

japow.title: Complete guide to pure powder—no chairlifts, no crowds
japow.body: Two rope tows, 100% advanced skiers, 34-minute average stay—a national hill with no chairlifts and no lift lines. GS 620 m and SL 420 m, max 1,000 m, 25° pitch, Wed/Fri night skiing 18:00–20:00. The polar opposite of adjacent Tomamu mega-resort—short, intense Japow.

patronageGuide.title: Powder Patronage—zero-revenue model explained
patronageGuide.body: Keep villagers free while visitors donate via digital QR at ¥1,000/day or ¥10,000/season—sustain a zero-lift-revenue national hill as a "powder culture patronage circle." Extend ARPU through Secret Powder Experience and mobile rental bundling (LP strategy note).

ecosystem.title: Tomamu contrast × village ecosystem model course
ecosystem.body: Narrow levee road ~3 km from Shimukappu Station, hours (Mon/Wed/Fri 14:00–20:00, Tue/Thu 14:00–18:00, Sat/Sun/holidays 13:00–18:00), Shizen Taikan Shimukappu, Yunosawa onsen ¥700, Topeni Wakka / Rupu no Mori—a village loop from 11:00–17:30 designed at ¥6,700–19,000 per visitor.
